$description: Machine-readable reference for AI agents (claude -p, codex exec, etc.) to autonomously use GSD workflows. Load this file to understand available commands, state detection, workflow sequencing, and error recovery.

meta.target_audience: AI agents running in non-interactive or semi-interactive mode

state_detection.description: Read these files to determine current project state before deciding which command to run.

files..planning/PROJECT.md: Project vision and context. EXISTS = project initialized.
files..planning/ROADMAP.md: Phase breakdown with status tracking. Read phase statuses here.
files..planning/STATE.md: Session memory, current position, blockers. Primary state source.

detect_current_phase.method: Read STATE.md frontmatter fields: active_phase, next_action, progress
detect_current_phase.fallback: Parse ROADMAP.md for first phase with status != 'completed'

detect_phase_executed.method: Check for *-SUMMARY.md files in phase directory

detect_phase_verified.method: Check for UAT.md or VERIFICATION.md with all checks passed

workflow_state_machine.description: The canonical sequence for completing a project. Each state has entry conditions and exit commands.

auto_advance.description: Use /gsd-progress --next to auto-detect and run the next step without manual state reading.

core_pipeline.name: /gsd-new-project
core_pipeline.purpose: Initialize project: questioning → research → requirements → roadmap
core_pipeline.agent_tip: Use --auto with a PRD file for non-interactive initialization.

core_pipeline.name: /gsd-discuss-phase
core_pipeline.agent_tip: Use --auto for non-interactive mode. Use --assumptions to surface Claude's assumptions without Q&A.

core_pipeline.name: /gsd-plan-phase
core_pipeline.agent_tip: Use --auto --skip-research for fastest non-interactive planning. Use --tdd for test-first order.

core_pipeline.name: /gsd-execute-phase
core_pipeline.purpose: Wave-based parallel execution of all plans in a phase
core_pipeline.agent_tip: Non-interactive by default. Each plan gets fresh 200k context executor. Commits are atomic.

core_pipeline.name: /gsd-verify-work
core_pipeline.purpose: Conversational UAT — extract testable deliverables and verify one by one
core_pipeline.agent_tip: Interactive by design. In non-interactive mode, agent should read SUMMARY.md and verify programmatically.

core_pipeline.name: /gsd-ship
core_pipeline.purpose: Push branch + create PR with auto-generated body
core_pipeline.agent_tip: Requires gh CLI authenticated. Use --draft for draft PRs.

core_pipeline.name: /gsd-progress
core_pipeline.purpose: Check progress and intelligently route to next action
core_pipeline.agent_tip: Use --next to auto-advance. Use --do "intent" for natural language routing to the right command.

quick_execution.name: /gsd-quick
quick_execution.purpose: Small ad-hoc tasks with GSD guarantees but skip optional agents
quick_execution.agent_tip: Default skips researcher/checker/verifier. Use --full for complete pipeline.

quick_execution.name: /gsd-fast
quick_execution.purpose: Trivial inline tasks — no subagents, no planning files
quick_execution.agent_tip: ≤ 3 file edits. Redirects to /gsd-quick if non-trivial.

milestone.name: /gsd-complete-milestone
milestone.purpose: Archive milestone and prepare for next version

milestone.name: /gsd-audit-milestone
milestone.purpose: Audit milestone completion against original intent

quality.name: /gsd-code-review
quality.purpose: Review phase source code for bugs/security/quality

quality.name: /gsd-validate-phase
quality.purpose: Retroactively audit and fill Nyquist validation gaps

diagnostics.name: /gsd-debug
diagnostics.agent_tip: Survives /clear. Run with no args to resume.

diagnostics.name: /gsd-health
diagnostics.agent_tip: --context shows context window utilization (60% warn, 70% critical)

exploration.name: /gsd-spike
exploration.agent_tip: No /gsd-new-project required. Use --wrap-up to package findings.

exploration.name: /gsd-sketch
exploration.agent_tip: No /gsd-new-project required. Use --wrap-up to package decisions.

exploration.name: /gsd-explore
exploration.purpose: Socratic ideation before committing to plans
exploration.output: Routes to appropriate artifact (note, todo, seed, phase)

context_knowledge.name: /gsd-map-codebase
context_knowledge.purpose: Parallel codebase analysis for brownfield projects

update_maintenance.name: /gsd-update
update_maintenance.agent_tip: Use --reapply after update to merge local modifications.

update_maintenance.name: /gsd-cleanup
update_maintenance.purpose: Archive phase directories from completed milestones

non_interactive_strategies.description: How to use GSD commands in agent contexts where human interaction is unavailable or undesirable.

strategy_1_auto_flags.description: Use --auto flags to skip interactive prompts

strategy_2_sdk_headless.description: Use GSD SDK CLI for fully autonomous execution

commands.gsd-sdk auto: Run entire milestone autonomously (discuss→plan→execute per phase)

example.init: const gsd = new GSD({ projectDir: '/path', autoMode: true, model: 'claude-opus-4-6', maxBudgetUsd: 10 });

strategy_4_progress_router.description: Use /gsd-progress as a smart router to dispatch intent to the right command
examples: /gsd-progress --do "I want to start a new milestone"

strategy_5_minimal_install.description: Install with --minimal to reduce system prompt overhead
strategy_5_minimal_install.effect: Cold-start from ~12k tokens to ~700. Only core skills installed.

decision_tree.description: Given a user intent, determine which GSD command to run. Read top-down, first match wins.

rules.condition: User wants to start from a PRD/requirements file
User describes a task to do
User wants to plan a phase
User wants to add/insert/remove a phase
User wants to capture an idea
User wants to know what to do next

error_recovery.description: Common failure patterns and recovery commands.

patterns.cause: Plan too ambitious (tasks exceed context window)

patterns.recovery: GSD auto-creates fix plan → /gsd-execute-phase N → re-verify

patterns.cause: Multiple agents triggering build tools simultaneously

patterns.recovery: Add required Bash patterns to ~/.claude/settings.json permissions.allow

mcp_cross_ai_integration.description: When the main controller (Claude TUI) uses MCP tools to call external AI (Codex, etc.) for review/discuss tasks instead of CLI subprocesses. Results return directly to Claude's context and can be followed up on.

architecture.pattern: Claude TUI (orchestrator) → GSD Skills (workflow) → MCP Tools (external AI) → .planning/ files (state)
architecture.key_difference_from_cli: MCP results return to Claude's context window directly; CLI results write to temp files. MCP enables follow-up questions; CLI is fire-and-forget.
architecture.state_truth_source: .planning/ files remain the single source of truth regardless of whether Claude or Codex provided the input.

plan_review_via_mcp.description: After /gsd-plan-phase, call MCP tool for independent plan review before execution.
steps: 3. Build structured review prompt (see template below)
4. Call MCP tool with review prompt → returns structured feedback
6. If HIGH concerns found: /gsd-plan-phase N --reviews → re-plan
plan_review_via_mcp.prompt_template: Review this implementation plan. Analyze: completeness, feasibility, risks, dependency ordering, and suggestions. Project context: {PROJECT.md}. Phase goal: {ROADMAP phase}. Requirements: {REQ-IDs}. Plans: {PLAN.md contents}. Decisions: {CONTEXT.md}.

code_review_via_mcp.description: After /gsd-execute-phase, call MCP tool for code review before verification.
steps: 2. Collect changed files from SUMMARY.md or git diff
3. Call MCP tool with code review prompt → returns findings
4. Classify findings: Critical (fix now), Warning (fix plan), Info (record)
6. Fix Critical issues directly or via /gsd-quick
code_review_via_mcp.prompt_template: Review this code for bugs, security issues, and quality problems. Files changed: {file list}. Code: {file contents}. Phase goal: {phase goal from ROADMAP}.

discuss_via_mcp.description: Before or after /gsd-discuss-phase, get independent perspective from external AI.
steps: 2. Call MCP tool: 'For this phase goal, what is the best implementation approach?'
3. Claude synthesizes its own view + Codex's view
discuss_via_mcp.prompt_template: I'm planning phase {N}: {phase goal}. Requirements: {REQ-IDs}. What is the optimal implementation approach? Consider: tech choices, patterns, edge cases, risks.

convergence_loop_via_mcp.description: Iterative review-replan loop until no HIGH concerns remain.
steps: 4. MCP re-review → check if HIGHs resolved
5. Repeat until clean or max 3 cycles

tool_selection.description: Available external AI integration methods. Codex uses slash commands (not MCP tools).

codex_plugin_commands.description: OpenAI Codex plugin — invoked as slash commands, not MCP tools

commands./codex:review: Code review against local git state. Flags: --wait, --background, --base <ref>, --scope auto|working-tree|branch
commands./codex:adversarial-review: Adversarial review that challenges implementation approach and design choices. Same flags as review + [focus ...]

modes./codex challenge: Adversarial mode that tries to break code
modes./codex consult: Ask codex anything with session continuity

gsd_native_cli.description: GSD built-in cross-AI review via CLI subprocess

mcp_advantages: Results in Claude context (can follow up)
Real-time synthesis of multiple AI views
vs_cli_mode.recommendation: Use MCP for interactive/iterative reviews. Use CLI for batch/automated reviews.

safety_budgets.description: Built-in safety mechanisms for autonomous execution.
safety_budgets.note: permissionMode: 'bypassPermissions' in SDK mode skips all confirmations. Use in sandboxed environments only.
